questions: Where exactly does each mutating surface write, and what is the dry-run seam?
Which surfaces force growth on the over-razor qor/cli.py?
How should session rotate get dry-run visibility without threading flags through automation?

findings.claim: Five surfaces are read-compute-write with 1-2 funnel points each; dry_run guards the writes while reads/validation still execute
findings.citation: seal_artifacts.py:106-113; cli_handlers/reconcile.py:31,69; reconcile.py:122; changelog_stamp.py:75; changelog_backends.py:49; governance_index.py:118; install.py:103,137

findings.claim: rotate() is invoked internally by ~4 modules mid-automation; ship an operator-facing session_tool (current/rotate --dry-run) and leave internal rotate mutation-only
Only reconcile (+2 flags, in cli_handlers) and uninstall (+1 flag) touch argparse outside module mains; install already has --dry-run
This phase completes #250 part b; parts a/c shipped in Phase 165 with live runtime evidence -- #250 closes at full scope
